mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from its prior month level raises the discount rate applied to corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0% from a lower level, it signals deteriorating household income expectations and triggers a rapid risk-off repricing that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances within 1 month after UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated contraction in aggregate demand and supply, amplifying downward earnings revision risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month declines in both RSAFS and INDPRO
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in RSAFS and INDPRO produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or interaction Rank IC exceeds -0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate during periods of elevated inflation compress equity valuations and cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0%, month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC less than -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal improving corporate profitability and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below its trailing four-quarter average signals persistent demand weakness that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls below trailing four-quarter average
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 is below its trailing four-quarter average.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment strengthen household income expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the data is priced in.
failure_condition: Positive changes in PAYEMS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,500 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1,500 thousand units.
